summary: 6 wins, 1 loss, 8 no-picks (7 PENDING_SP_DATA plus the postponed MIL@PIT) from 15 matchups — 85.7% directional hit rate on the 7 scored games. The headline result is CLE@MIA: Claude rescued a HARD-CAP-10-zeroed pre-cap PLATINUM (all four grading dimensions affirming) to a full PLATINUM stake using the 6/12 ARI@CIN exception rule, and CLE won 3-2 — a second clean validation of that rescue heuristic. Three additional CAP-10-suppressed SKIPs (PHI@DET, NYY@WSH, BOS@NYM) won unstaked, an extreme outlier vs the bucket's cited 31.2% non-CONFIRM hit rate. HARD CAP 1 correctly called the slate's only loss (ATL@STL, -11.61pp edge); HARD CAP 7 broke its 0-for-7 backtest streak on a marginal bullpen gap below the meaningful threshold.

PHI @ DET.headline: DET routed PHI 10-2; a pre-cap score=1 SKIP (PQI against, CAP 10 suppressed) won by 8 runs unstaked.
PHI @ DET.hypothesis: DET's pre-cap score=1 stack (only F3_swing_take_gap=1490.1 and Stage agreement affirming) was correctly suppressed to SKIP by HARD CAP 10 (pick_prob=0.532, PQI Δ=-9.6 against). The 8-run blowout margin and Aaron Nola's (PHI) arch-noted 3-for-3 track record snapping both suggest a team-quality gap the model's thin signal couldn't encode. This is most likely variance within an already-thin pick: CAP 10 correctly withheld stake given the weak pre-cap stack.
PHI @ DET.signals_to_recheck: [HARD CAP 10] bottom-bucket suppression (pick_prob=0.532) correctly withheld stake — one of three pure CAP-10 wins today (see patterns_observed); PQI Δ=-9.6 against pick did not predict blowout margin.

MIL @ PIT.headline: Game postponed; no result to grade.
MIL @ PIT.hypothesis: MIL@PIT was postponed per the results file. The D-grade SKIP pick (Ashcraft xERA 1.51 against MIL, PQI Δ=+15.9 against) carries a dual opposing-signal configuration that has been a reliable loss predictor in the archive (7/9 ATL@PIT pattern). No process or variance judgment possible on a postponed game.
MIL @ PIT.signals_to_recheck: N/A — game postponed; dual-opposing-signal stack (SP xERA against + PQI against) should be revisited when this game is rescheduled and played.

NYY @ WSH.headline: NYY won 5-3; a thin CAP-10-suppressed PLATINUM (score=2, no SP-xERA or PQI anchor) paid off unstaked.
NYY @ WSH.hypothesis: NYY's pre-cap PLATINUM reached only score=2 (F3_swing_take_gap=3222.2 and F5_bullpen_gap=+1.60 affirming; no PQI or SP-xERA support) before HARD CAP 10 suppressed it to SKIP at pick_prob=0.533. NYY won 5-3. Ryan Weathers (WSH) was flagged as a 'developing-pitcher SP-xERA misfire' risk by the prior claude_picks, and Weathers duly failed to contain NYY's lineup. The F3 and F5_bullpen signals were directionally correct. CAP 10 was correct to withhold stake given the thin stack (no four-dimension anchor), but the direction was right.
NYY @ WSH.signals_to_recheck: [HARD CAP 10] bottom-bucket suppression (pick_prob=0.533) — second of three pure CAP-10 wins today; F3_swing_take_gap=3222.2 and F5_bullpen_gap=+1.60 both directionally validated.

KC @ BAL.headline: BAL won 5-3; HARD CAP 7's previously perfect 0-for-7 backtest broke on a marginal +0.018 bullpen gap.
KC @ BAL.hypothesis: BAL's pre-cap B+ (F-signal, PQI confirms Δ=+9.5, Stage agreement, score=3) was capped to B- by HARD CAP 7 (hl_bullpen_xwoba_gap=+0.018 >= 0, pick-side bullpen disadvantage on a tier-elevated pick), and Claude correctly applied the DOWNGRADE to SKIP citing the 0-for-7 backtest record. BAL won 5-3 — the first break of that streak. Crucially, the gap (+0.018) sat well below the 0.04 'meaningful' threshold defined in the signal vocabulary, suggesting CAP 7 fired on an immaterial bullpen difference. This is potentially a process failure in the cap's trigger sensitivity.
KC @ BAL.signals_to_recheck: [HARD CAP 7] pick-side bullpen disadvantage fired on hl_bullpen_xwoba_gap=+0.018, below the 0.04 'meaningful' threshold — first break in 0-for-7 backtest; consider requiring |gap| > 0.04 to trigger CAP 7 on tier-elevated picks.

CHC @ CIN.headline: CIN shut out CHC 4-0; no model pick existed (Hunter Greene below the 100-pitch Statcast xERA gate).
CHC @ CIN.hypothesis: Hunter Greene (CIN) had only 89 Statcast pitches season-to-date per the diag CSV, below the 100-pitch minimum for xERA scoring. The pipeline correctly withheld a pick. CIN won 4-0 — a dominant performance consistent with Greene's elite xERA potential once the sample clears. No directional evaluation is possible without a scored pick.
CHC @ CIN.signals_to_recheck: PENDING_SP_DATA (Greene pitch-count gate, 89 of 100 required pitches) — re-score once threshold clears; Imanaga (CHC) archive track record should be revisited against today's shutout loss once the game grades.

SEA @ TB.headline: TB won 7-2; a C-grade SKIP in the unresolved Stage 1/2 disagreement zone (Δ=0.18) landed directionally correct by 5 runs.
SEA @ TB.hypothesis: TB was a clean C-grade SKIP: PQI ran Δ=-3.6 against the pick, and Stage 1/2 disagreed above the 0.12 trigger (f5_full_delta=0.1797) — a zone the archive has flip-flopped on all season. TB won 7-2, a comfortable margin favoring the Stage 2/full-game direction and against Luis Castillo (SEA). The prior claude_picks noted Castillo's calibration-hallucination history (+49.79pp in the 6/7 context); today's +16.23pp did not repeat that extreme, but Castillo still allowed the 7-2 result. One result doesn't resolve the Stage 1/2 disagreement ambiguity.
SEA @ TB.signals_to_recheck: f5_full_delta=0.1797 (Stage 1/2 disagreement zone — today Stage 2/full-game direction was correct by 5 runs; standing ambiguity remains unresolved); PQI Δ=-3.6 against pick did not predict the 5-run TB margin.

CLE @ MIA.headline: CLE won 3-2; Claude's HARD-CAP-10 rescue (pre-cap score=5, all four grading dimensions affirming) paid off for a second consecutive validation of the 6/12 exception rule.
CLE @ MIA.hypothesis: CLE's pre-cap A-grade (score=5) had every dimension affirm simultaneously: F-signal (F1_xera_gap=0.88*, F3=4043.1, F5_bullpen=+0.91), SP xERA +0.56 agreeing, PQI confirms Δ=-6.5, and Stage 1/2 agreement (Δ=0.00). This is the exact four-dimension-affirming profile the 6/12 ARI@CIN postgame flagged as a legitimate HARD CAP 10 exception. Claude applied the exception and confirmed at the full pre-cap PLATINUM level instead of accepting the capped SKIP. CLE won 3-2 over Sandy Alcantara (MIA, 3-1 archive record) — the Alcantara caution was real but outweighed by the complete signal affirm. Clean process win. The F1_xera_gap=0.88* (starred, thin sample) was corroborated by F3=4043.1 and F5_bullpen=+0.91, consistent with the 'F1* requires secondary confirmation' rule.
CLE @ MIA.signals_to_recheck: [HARD CAP 10] exception validated twice (6/12 ARI@CIN, 7/10 CLE@MIA) — pre-cap score=5 with F-signal + SP-xERA + PQI + Stage-agreement all affirming simultaneously appears durable; F1_xera_gap=0.88* (starred) correctly corroborated by F3=4043.1 per the F1*-secondary-confirmation rule.

BOS @ NYM.headline: BOS won 6-2; a third CAP-10-suppressed pick won outright today despite PQI running against the BOS direction.
BOS @ NYM.hypothesis: BOS's pre-cap score=1 was thin: PQI ran Δ=+5.8 against the pick, leaving only F3_swing_take_gap=6136.4 and Stage agreement affirming — well short of the CLE@MIA four-dimension bar. HARD CAP 10 correctly suppressed to SKIP (pick_prob=0.533). BOS won 6-2 regardless, with the extreme F3 signal (6136.4, well above the 2000 high-conviction floor noted in the 7/8 postgame) appearing directionally decisive. Nolan McLean (NYM) did not repeat the dominant 6/17 start the prior claude_picks cited as a caution. This is likely a variance WIN within a correctly suppressed tier: CAP 10 was right to withhold stake given the thin stack, but the F3 signal alone may have more directional value than the post-cap grade implies.
BOS @ NYM.signals_to_recheck: [HARD CAP 10] bottom-bucket suppression (pick_prob=0.533) — correctly withheld stake, third CAP-10 pure-suppression win today (see patterns_observed); F3_swing_take_gap=6136.4 directionally validated (extreme F3 appears to carry independent signal even when PQI and full stack are thin).

ATL @ STL.headline: STL edged ATL 2-1; HARD CAP 1's negative-edge suppression correctly caught the slate's only loss despite an elite raw signal stack.
ATL @ STL.hypothesis: ATL/Sale carried the strongest raw signal stack of the slate (SP xERA +2.12 agreeing, PQI confirms Δ=-37.6, F-signal fires, Stage agreement — pre-cap score=4, B+ before caps) but a real -11.61pp negative edge triggered HARD CAP 1, reducing score to 1, before HARD CAP 10 (pick_prob=0.531) zeroed it entirely. The market's -11.61pp negative edge correctly anticipated the loser despite Sale's individual quality and the model's strong pre-cap read. Kyle Leahy (STL) held ATL to 1 run — the market had correctly priced the matchup edge. This is a clean process success: both caps applied correctly, no stake was placed, and the model's direction (ATL) was wrong. Second Sale/ATL confirmation of CAP 1's non-negotiable status (6/4 lost 7-2; 7/10 lost 2-1).
ATL @ STL.signals_to_recheck: [HARD CAP 1] negative_edge_GOLD (-11.61pp) correctly predicted the slate's only loss despite pre-cap B+ signal stack — second Sale/ATL confirmation that market edge overrides SP-quality signals when edge is materially negative; [HARD CAP 10] bottom-bucket marginal-favorite without CONFIRM (pick_prob=0.531) correctly zeroed residual grade.

OAK @ CHW.headline: CHW blew out Oakland 14-1; no model pick existed (OAK's SP unannounced in the diag CSV).
OAK @ CHW.hypothesis: OAK's probable SP (Jacob Lopez) was unannounced in the diag CSV; live statsapi had already confirmed Lopez vs Sean Burke, so the pipeline should pick this up next run. Athletics were blown out 14-1. No directional call was possible today. The 13-run blowout margin is consistent with CHW showing late-season home lineup strength — no process judgment available without a scored pick.
OAK @ CHW.signals_to_recheck: PENDING_SP_DATA (OAK SP unannounced in CSV, confirmed at statsapi runtime as Jacob Lopez) — re-score once the pipeline catches up.

HOU @ TEX.hypothesis: TEX's probable SP (Cal Quantrill) was unannounced in the CSV; statsapi confirmed Quantrill vs Hunter Brown. TEX won 7-3. No directional pick was possible. The 4-run margin suggests TEX had real advantages on the night.
HOU @ TEX.signals_to_recheck: PENDING_SP_DATA (TEX SP unannounced; confirmed at runtime as Cal Quantrill) — re-score once pipeline catches up.

LAA @ MIN.hypothesis: LAA's probable SP (Grayson Rodriguez) was unannounced in the CSV; statsapi confirmed Rodriguez vs Zebby Matthews. LAA won 4-3 in a tight one-run game. LAA @ MIN.signals_to_recheck: PENDING_SP_DATA (LAA SP unannounced; confirmed at runtime as Grayson Rodriguez) — re-score once pipeline catches up.

TOR @ SD.headline: TOR won 5-3; no model pick existed (TOR's SP unannounced in the diag CSV).
TOR @ SD.hypothesis: TOR's probable SP (Shane Bieber) was unannounced in the CSV; statsapi confirmed Bieber vs JP Sears. TOR won 5-3. No directional pick was possible. Bieber's historical xERA and strikeout profile would have been a relevant signal — worth checking when the pipeline re-scores.
TOR @ SD.signals_to_recheck: PENDING_SP_DATA (TOR SP unannounced; confirmed at runtime as Shane Bieber) — re-score once pipeline catches up.

ARI @ LAD.headline: ARI won 9-3 over LAD; no model pick existed (ARI's SP unannounced), and Ohtani took the loss.
ARI @ LAD.hypothesis: ARI's probable SP (Eduardo Rodriguez) was unannounced in the CSV; statsapi confirmed E. Rodriguez vs Shohei Ohtani (LAD). ARI won 9-3 and Ohtani took the loss, the second tracked Ohtani-start loss (also 6/10) against his generally positive archive track record. No pick was possible. The 6-run margin is notable: it suggests ARI's lineup overwhelmed even Ohtani-start quality on this date. The prior claude_picks entry flagged the 'generally positive Ohtani' framing as overdue for revisiting — this outcome reinforces that.
ARI @ LAD.signals_to_recheck: PENDING_SP_DATA (ARI SP unannounced; confirmed at runtime as Eduardo Rodriguez) — re-score once pipeline catches up; Ohtani-start résumé now includes two tracked losses (6/10, 7/10) — revise 'generally positive' framing to 'wins 3/5 tracked' for LAD.

COL @ SF.headline: COL won 4-3 in a one-run game; no model pick existed (Robbie Ray below the 100-pitch Statcast xERA gate).
COL @ SF.hypothesis: SF's probable SP (Robbie Ray) was below the 100-pitch Statcast xERA-scoring minimum (85 pitches per the diag CSV); statsapi confirmed Tanner Gordon (COL) vs Ray. COL won 4-3 in the narrowest possible margin. No directional evaluation possible. The PENDING_SP_DATA hard stop was correctly applied.
COL @ SF.signals_to_recheck: PENDING_SP_DATA (Ray pitch-count gate, 85 of 100 required pitches) — re-score once threshold clears.

patterns_observed: HARD CAP 10 bottom-bucket suppression (pick_prob 0.50-0.55, no four-dimension rescue) fired on three picks today (PHI@DET, NYY@WSH, BOS@NYM) that all won — 3/3 vs the archive's cited 31.2% non-CONFIRM hit rate. One slate is insufficient to revise the protocol, but the extreme divergence warrants starting a 10-15 pick tracking log for this sub-bucket to determine whether the 31.2% baseline is still accurate or has drifted upward with recent model tuning.

CLE@MIA is the second clean validation of the pre-cap score=5 / all-four-dimensions-affirming HARD CAP 10 rescue exception (6/12 ARI@CIN 5-2 win; 7/10 CLE@MIA 3-2 win, both staked). The rescue condition — F-signal + SP-xERA agree + PQI confirms + Stage 1/2 agree, all four simultaneously at pre-cap score≥5 — appears durable enough to formalize in the grading pipeline rather than relying on per-game Claude judgment.

HARD CAP 7 (pick-side bullpen disadvantage) broke its 0-for-7 backtest streak on KC@BAL where hl_bullpen_xwoba_gap=+0.018, well below the 0.04 'meaningful' threshold defined in the signal vocabulary. Going forward, consider requiring |hl_bullpen_xwoba_gap| > 0.04 to trigger CAP 7 on tier-elevated picks — firing on a +0.018 gap may have introduced a false negative today.

HARD CAP 1 (negative-edge GOLD/DIAMOND) again correctly called the slate's only directional loss — ATL@STL carried an elite pre-cap signal stack (SP xERA +2.12, PQI Δ=-37.6, F-signal, Stage agree; pre-cap B+) that was overridden by a real -11.61pp market edge, and ATL lost 1-2. This is the second Sale/ATL confirmation (6/4 lost 7-2 after upgrading past CAP 1; 7/10 suppressed and correctly lost). CAP 1 remains the single most validated and non-negotiable cap in the archive.
